action.reasoning: Observation: the intended DLinear switch was only partially applied; model.py defines DLinearForecaster, but train.py and predict.py still import/use LinearForecaster, so the workspace is inconsistent and would fail on the next run. Analysis: first priority is to repair the pipeline, then train the DLinear model for a moderate number of epochs and submit eval. CPU training previously took ~154s for 5 epochs with the simpler model, so ~12 epochs with this lightweight model should still fit comfortably. Decision: rewrite train.py and predict.py to use DLinearForecaster, keep robust checkpointing and normalization, run a local smoke test, then submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 12 --batch_size 256 --lr 3e-3 --device cpu && python predict.py --data_path smoke_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output smoke_test/out.json --device cpu
